steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: The problem defines force as the product of mass and acceleration: $$F = m \cdot a$$

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: Substitute the given mass of 27 kg and acceleration $a$ m/s$^2$: $$F = 27 \cdot a = 27a$$

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content:
Evaluate each choice:
  • A: $F = 27a$ — Force equals mass times acceleration with mass = 27. This is exactly what the formula gives. That confirms A is correct.
  • B: $F = 27 + a$ — Uses addition instead of multiplication; the problem says "product," not sum. Eliminated.
  • C: $F = 27 - a$ — Uses subtraction; unrelated to the definition given. Eliminated.
  • D: $F = a - 27$ — Uses subtraction in the wrong order; unrelated to the definition given. Eliminated.
